---
title: "kinship"
author: "Andrea Estandia"
date: "30/03/2026"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup}
# Knitr settings: 
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = rprojroot::find_rstudio_root_file())
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, eval = FALSE)
options(scipen = 999)
```

```{r}
source("./src/wm-europe_source.R")
```

```{r}
metadata <-  read.csv(
  file.path(data_path, "dfs_processed", "wgs_europe_metadata.csv"),
  header = TRUE
) %>%
 rename(IID=sample_name)
```

```{r}
# =============================================================================
# Isolation by distance: PI_HAT (IBD) and KING kinship heatmap
# All PI_HAT values are derived from the full-dataset genome file only,
# so allele frequencies are always estimated from the complete sample.
# =============================================================================

# --- Individuals to exclude --------------------------------------------------
remove_ind <- c(
  "CHA_M66", "MCH_F7", "BNS05", "CHS012", "CMB028", "CMN08",
  "DUM2", "GUL1", "GUL3", "GUL9", "OSL1", "OSL12", "ROS18", "SP4", "SP5"
)

# --- Metadata ----------------------------------------------------------------
metadata_filt <- metadata %>%
  filter(!IID %in% remove_ind)

meta1 <- metadata_filt %>% rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_1"), -IID)
meta2 <- metadata_filt %>% rename_with(~ paste0(.x, "_2"), -IID)

# =============================================================================
# IBD (PI_HAT) — full dataset genome file, subsetted after joining
# Shetland, Spain, and Norway excluded — sample sizes too small for reliable
# allele frequency estimation, which inflates PI_HAT (use KING kinship instead).
# =============================================================================

ibd_combined <- read.table(
  file.path(reports_path, "ibd", "wm_europe_biallelic_filtered_pruned_all.genome"),
  header = TRUE
) %>%
  filter(!IID1 %in% remove_ind, !IID2 %in% remove_ind) %>%
  mutate(
    sample_a = pmin(IID1, IID2),
    sample_b = pmax(IID1, IID2)
  ) %>%
  select(sample_a, sample_b, RT, EZ, Z0, Z1, Z2, PI_HAT, PHE, DST, PPC, RATIO) %>%
  left_join(meta1, by = c("sample_a" = "IID")) %>%
  left_join(meta2, by = c("sample_b" = "IID")) %>%
  mutate(
    distance_km = distHaversine(
      cbind(longitude_1, latitude_1),
      cbind(longitude_2, latitude_2)
    ) / 1000
  )

ibd_subset_defs <- list(
  all = function(df) df,
  uk  = function(df) df %>% filter(country_1 == "UK" & country_2 == "UK")
)

# Build both datasets first
df_all <- ibd_subset_defs$all(ibd_combined)
df_uk  <- ibd_subset_defs$uk(ibd_combined)

get_summary <- function(df) {
  df %>%
    filter(!is.na(distance_km), !is.na(PI_HAT)) %>%
    mutate(bin_start = floor(distance_km / 100) * 100) %>%
    group_by(bin_start) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_pi  = mean(PI_HAT, na.rm = TRUE),
      n        = n(),
      se       = sd(PI_HAT, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
      ci_lower = mean_pi - 1.96 * se,
      ci_upper = mean_pi + 1.96 * se,
      .groups  = "drop"
    )
}

sum_all <- get_summary(df_all)
sum_uk  <- get_summary(df_uk)

y_min <- min(c(sum_all$ci_lower, sum_uk$ci_lower), na.rm = TRUE)
y_max <- max(c(sum_all$ci_upper, sum_uk$ci_upper), na.rm = TRUE)

y_limits <- range(
  c(sum_all$ci_lower, sum_all$ci_upper,
    sum_uk$ci_lower,  sum_uk$ci_upper),
  na.rm = TRUE
)

make_ibd_plot <- function(df, subset_name, title_text) {

  df_plot <- df %>%
    filter(!is.na(distance_km), !is.na(PI_HAT)) %>%
    mutate(bin_start = floor(distance_km / 100) * 100) %>%
    group_by(bin_start) %>%
    summarise(
      mean_pi  = mean(PI_HAT, na.rm = TRUE),
      n        = n(),
      se       = sd(PI_HAT, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n),
      ci_lower = mean_pi - 1.96 * se,
      ci_upper = mean_pi + 1.96 * se,
      .groups  = "drop"
    )

  if (nrow(df_plot) == 0) {
    message("No usable data for IBD subset: ", subset_name)
    return(NULL)
  }

  x_max <- max(df_plot$bin_start, na.rm = TRUE)

  p <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = bin_start, y = mean_pi)) +
    geom_point(size = 4) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper), width = 50) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = seq(0, x_max, by = 100),
      labels = label_number(accuracy = 1, big.mark = "")
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = y_limits) +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(
      panel.grid       = element_blank(),
      panel.background = element_blank(),
      text             = element_text(family = "roboto_condensed", size = 14),
      plot.title       = element_text(hjust = 0, face = "bold", size = 14)
    ) +
    labs(
      title = title_text,
      x = "\nDistance (km)",
      y = "Mean IBD \u00b1 95% CI\n"
    )

  p
}

ibd_plots <- list(
  all = make_ibd_plot(ibd_subset_defs$all(ibd_combined), "all", "All samples"),
  uk  = make_ibd_plot(ibd_subset_defs$uk(ibd_combined),  "uk",  "UK mainland")
)

names(ibd_plots) <- names(ibd_subset_defs)

combined_plot <- cowplot::plot_grid(
  ibd_plots$all,
  ibd_plots$uk,
  labels = c("A", "B"),
  rel_widths = c(1.5, 1)  # A is 1.5× wider than B
)

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(figures_path, "ibd_distance_combined.png"),
  plot     = combined_plot,
  width    = 16,
  height   = 5,
  dpi      = 300
)

# =============================================================================
# Kinship (KING) — pairwise heatmap
# =============================================================================

kinship_raw <- read.table(
  file.path(reports_path, "kinship", "wm_europe_biallelic_filtered.kin0"),
  header = TRUE
) %>%
  filter(!ID1 %in% remove_ind, !ID2 %in% remove_ind) %>%
  mutate(KINSHIP = ifelse(KINSHIP < 0, 0, KINSHIP)) 

# Make symmetric: duplicate each pair with IDs swapped so the matrix is full
kinship_sym <- bind_rows(
  kinship_raw %>% select(ID1, ID2, KINSHIP),
  kinship_raw %>% rename(ID1 = ID2, ID2 = ID1) %>% select(ID1, ID2, KINSHIP)
)

# Order samples by population then ID for readable axis labels
sample_order <- metadata_filt %>%
  arrange(country, IID) %>%
  pull(IID)

kinship_sym <- kinship_sym %>%
  mutate(
    ID1 = factor(ID1, levels = sample_order),
    ID2 = factor(ID2, levels = sample_order)
  )

p_kinship <- ggplot(kinship_sym, aes(x = ID1, y = ID2, fill = KINSHIP)) +
  geom_tile() +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "#4575b4",
    mid      = "white",
    high     = "#d73027",
    midpoint = 0,
    name     = "Kinship"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x     = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 5),
    axis.text.y     = element_text(size = 5),
    axis.title      = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "right",
    text            = element_text(family = "roboto_condensed", size = 14)
  )

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(figures_path, "kinship_heatmap.png"),
  plot     = p_kinship,
  width    = 10,
  height   = 9,
  dpi      = 300
)
```
